name: City Sky
milkyWayVisibility: None — entire sky is brightly lit; only bright planets and a few dozen stars visible
description: Typical city skies. The sky glows white or orange-white. Only the brightest stars — perhaps 50 — are visible even on clear nights. Familiar constellations like Orion are recognizable but incomplete, missing fainter component stars. The sky is bright enough to read by in many areas. Deep-sky astronomy is essentially impossible without electronic aids. This is the normal experience for most urban dwellers worldwide. Over 2 billion people live under Class 8 or worse skies. Artificial lighting from street lamps, buildings, and signage completely dominates the natural sky.
